title: Enphase Energy Monetizes $235 Million in Federal Tax Credits for Domestic Strategy
text: Enphase Energy introduced U.S.-made residential and commercial products to bolster available federal tax credits for customers. When paired with U.S.-made solar racking, projects qualify for Domestic Content Bonus tax credits offering additional 10% atop 30% current credits through IRA with extra 10% in low-income communities allowing total potential tax credit up to 50% for installations. Enphase aggressively pivoted manufacturing strategy mid-2024 to meet Domestic Content Bonus requirements ramping production in U.S. to accrue Section 45X credits for every microinverter produced on American soil. U.S. government escalated Section 301 tariffs on Chinese solar components to 50% in late 2024 with subsequent energy duties reaching 60% early 2025. Company attributed gross margin decline to tariffs as battery cells sourced from China tariffed at 145%. For Enphase, 45X credit acts as margin bridge allowing sale of U.S.-made microinverters at competitive prices with subsidized imports while maintaining profitability. $235 million monetization event began mid-2024 with manufacturing pivot and 7% credit sale discount expected to shave 6.7 percentage points off Q1 2026 GAAP gross margins.
